title: Tonkotsu Ramen
description: A rich and savory Japanese noodle soup dish, made with a pork tonkotsu broth and topped with various ingredients.

ingredients.name: Pork bones
ingredients.notes: You can use pork neck bones or rib bones for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Tonkotsu broth
ingredients.notes: You can use store-bought tonkotsu broth or make your own by simmering pork bones in water.

ingredients.name: Ramen noodles
ingredients.notes: You can use fresh or dried ramen noodles for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Sliced pork
ingredients.notes: You can use pork loin or pork belly for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Green onions
ingredients.notes: You can use thinly sliced green onions for garnish.

ingredients.name: Sesame seeds
ingredients.notes: You can use sesame seeds for garnish.

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: You can use soy sauce for seasoning.

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.notes: You can use sesame oil for seasoning.

ingredients.name: Ground white pepper
ingredients.notes: You can use ground white pepper for seasoning.

ingredients.name: Pickled ginger
ingredients.notes: You can use pickled ginger for garnish.

instructions: 1. In a large pot, combine pork bones and tonkotsu broth. Bring to a boil over high heat.
2. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 4-6 hours, or until the broth is rich and flavorful.
3. Cook ramen noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
4. Slice pork into thin strips. Season with soy sauce, sesame oil, and ground white pepper.
5. In a large bowl, combine cooked noodles, sliced pork, and green onions.
6. Ladle hot broth over the top of the noodles. Garnish with sesame seeds and pickled ginger.
